artPrompt: A robed reckoner of bone and ash stands on a darkened porch, one skeletal hand raised mid-knock at a peeling door, its hooded face hidden in shadow against a blood-dark sky. A single cold-gold gleam from a hanging lantern rakes the doorframe; smoke curls at its feet. The landscape composition towers the figure over a small huddled house, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Door-to-Door Reckoning
text: Each player sacrifices a creature with the least power among creatures they control. Then put a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock for each creature sacrificed this way. The reckoning visits every household equally.
flavorText: It knocks once. There is no second knock.
